parts.title: SKILL 7: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS
parts.instruction: Negative expressions are very common in the short dialogues, and the most common kind of correct response to a negative statement is a positive statement containing a word with an opposite meaning.

questions.passage:
(man) Are you going to take out the trash?
(woman) I have no time to do it.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She is very busy.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the woman's statement 'I have no time to do it' is a direct paraphrase of being very busy. The phrase 'no time' is synonymous with being busy.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan wanita 'I have no time to do it' adalah parafrase langsung dari 'sangat sibuk'. Frasa 'no time' identik dengan sibuk.

options.text: She has lots of free time.

reason.en: This is the opposite of what the woman means. She explicitly states she has 'no time'.
reason.id: Ini adalah kebalikan dari apa yang dimaksud wanita itu. Dia secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa dia 'tidak punya waktu'.

options.text: It is not necessary to take out the trash.

reason.en: The woman's statement is about her lack of time, not about the necessity of the task.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita itu adalah tentang kurangnya waktu, bukan tentang keharusan tugas tersebut.

options.text: She will do it if she has time.

reason.en: While this might be true in a general sense, her statement 'I have no time' implies she cannot do it *now* because she is busy, not that she will do it if she finds time later.
reason.id: Meskipun ini mungkin benar secara umum, pernyataannya 'I have no time' menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak bisa melakukannya *sekarang* karena dia sibuk, bukan bahwa dia akan melakukannya jika dia punya waktu nanti.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(woman) Are you worried about the interview?
(man) It's unimportant to me.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The interview is very important.

reason.en: This answer is the opposite of what the man implies. He states the interview is 'unimportant,' which directly contradicts this option.
reason.id: Jawaban ini adalah kebalikan dari apa yang tersirat dari pria itu. Dia menyatakan wawancara itu 'tidak penting', yang secara langsung bertentangan dengan pilihan ini.

options.text: He is worried about the interview.

reason.en: The man explicitly states the interview is 'unimportant to me,' which means he is not worried. This answer is a direct contradiction.
reason.id: Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa wawancara itu 'tidak penting bagiku', yang berarti dia tidak khawatir. Jawaban ini adalah kontradiksi langsung.

options.text: What he's wearing to the interview is important.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention anything about what the man is wearing to the interview. This is an irrelevant detail and a potential trap.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak menyebutkan apa pun tentang apa yang dikenakan pria itu untuk wawancara. Ini adalah detail yang tidak relevan dan potensi jebakan.

options.text: He is not concerned about the interview.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, the man uses the word 'unimportant' to express a lack of concern. This is a direct paraphrase of not being concerned about the interview.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, pria itu menggunakan kata 'tidak penting' untuk mengekspresikan kurangnya perhatian. Ini adalah parafrase langsung dari tidak peduli tentang wawancara.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you have all the notes for Psychol ogy 101?
(man) I didn't miss a single lecture.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He has almost all the notes.

reason.en: The man states he didn't miss a single lecture, which means he attended all of them, not 'almost all'. This option is incorrect.
reason.id: Pria itu menyatakan bahwa dia tidak melewatkan satu pun kuliah, yang berarti dia menghadiri semuanya, bukan 'hampir semua'. Pilihan ini salah.

options.text: His attendance was perfect.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, the man's statement 'I didn't miss a single lecture' directly implies perfect attendance. This is the most accurate interpretation of the last line.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, pernyataan pria 'I didn't miss a single lecture' secara langsung menyiratkan kehadiran yang sempurna. Ini adalah interpretasi yang paling akurat dari baris terakhir.

options.text: He went to all the lectures but one.

reason.en: The phrase 'didn't miss a single lecture' means zero missed lectures, not one missed lecture. This option contradicts the man's statement.
reason.id: Frasa 'didn't miss a single lecture' berarti nol kuliah yang terlewat, bukan satu kuliah yang terlewat. Pilihan ini bertentangan dengan pernyataan pria itu.

options.text: He missed more than one psychology class.

reason.en: This option directly contradicts the man's statement that he 'didn't miss a single lecture'.
reason.id: Pilihan ini secara langsung bertentangan dengan pernyataan pria itu bahwa dia 'tidak melewatkan satu pun kuliah'.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 1 - Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you know the library's summer hours? I need to go there this evening.
(man) It's never open past six o'clock in the summer.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: They passed the library at 6:00.

reason.en: The man's statement implies the library is closed at 6:00 PM, not that they passed it at that time. This option misinterprets the meaning of 'open past six o'clock'.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria menyiratkan bahwa perpustakaan tutup pada pukul 18:00, bukan bahwa mereka melewatinya pada waktu itu. Opsi ini salah menafsirkan arti 'open past six o'clock'.

options.text: The library opens at 6:00 in the summer.

reason.en: The man says the library is 'never open past six o'clock,' which means it closes at or before 6:00 PM, not that it opens at 6:00 PM.
reason.id: Pria itu mengatakan perpustakaan 'tidak pernah buka melewati jam enam', yang berarti perpustakaan tutup pada atau sebelum pukul 18:00, bukan buka pada pukul 18:00.

options.text: The library closes at 6:00.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, the phrase 'never open past six o'clock' is a negative statement that implies the opposite: the library closes at 6:00 PM. This is a common way to express closing time using a negative.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, frasa 'tidak pernah buka melewati jam enam' adalah pernyataan negatif yang menyiratkan kebalikannya: perpustakaan tutup pada pukul 18:00. Ini adalah cara umum untuk menyatakan waktu tutup menggunakan negasi.

options.text: You can't check out more than six books in the summer.

reason.en: The dialogue is about the library's hours of operation, not about the number of books that can be checked out. This option introduces an irrelevant detail.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut membahas jam operasional perpustakaan, bukan tentang jumlah buku yang dapat dipinjam. Opsi ini memperkenalkan detail yang tidak relevan.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(woman) How often should I water the plants while you're gone?
(man) No more than once a week.
questions.question: 5. WHAT SHOULD THE WOMAN DO?

options.text: Water the plants once a day.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because the man explicitly states 'No more than once a week,' which contradicts watering 'once a day.'
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan 'Tidak lebih dari seminggu sekali,' yang bertentangan dengan menyiram 'sekali sehari.'

options.text: Give the plants no more water.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect. The man says 'No more than once a week,' which implies that watering once a week is acceptable, not that the plants should receive no water at all.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah. Pria itu mengatakan 'Tidak lebih dari seminggu sekali,' yang menyiratkan bahwa menyiram seminggu sekali dapat diterima, bukan bahwa tanaman tidak boleh disiram sama sekali.

options.text: Water the plants often while the man is gone.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect. The word 'often' implies frequent watering, which is the opposite of the man's instruction 'No more than once a week.'
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah. Kata 'sering' menyiratkan penyiraman yang sering, yang merupakan kebalikan dari instruksi pria itu 'Tidak lebih dari seminggu sekali.'

options.text: Give the plants a limited amount of water.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, this is the correct answer. The man's statement 'No more than once a week' means that the watering should be limited, and option [3] paraphrases this by stating 'Give the plants a limited amount of water.'
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, ini adalah jawaban yang benar. Pernyataan pria itu 'Tidak lebih dari seminggu sekali' berarti penyiraman harus dibatasi, dan pilihan [3] memparafrasekan ini dengan menyatakan 'Berikan tanaman jumlah air yang terbatas.'

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(man) Did you enjoy your meal? That restau rant is very famous.
(woman) The food was good, but I was dissatis fied with the service.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The service satisfied her.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the woman explicitly states she was 'dissatisfied' with the service, which is the opposite of satisfied.
reason.id: Ini salah karena wanita itu secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa dia 'tidak puas' dengan layanan tersebut, yang merupakan kebalikan dari puas.

options.text: The food was worse than the service.

reason.en: This is incorrect. While the woman was dissatisfied with the service, she also stated 'The food was good.' This option incorrectly implies the food was worse than the service.
reason.id: Ini salah. Meskipun wanita itu tidak puas dengan layanan, dia juga menyatakan 'Makanannya enak.' Pilihan ini secara keliru menyiratkan bahwa makanannya lebih buruk daripada layanannya.

options.text: She thought the service was bad.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, the woman's statement 'I was dissatisfied with the service' directly means that she thought the service was bad. 'Dissatisfied' is a negative expression indicating unhappiness or displeasure.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, pernyataan wanita 'Saya tidak puas dengan layanan' secara langsung berarti bahwa dia menganggap layanan itu buruk. 'Tidak puas' adalah ekspresi negatif yang menunjukkan ketidakbahagiaan atau ketidaksenangan.

options.text: Neither the food nor the service was satisfying.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The woman clearly states, 'The food was good.' This option wrongly claims that neither the food nor the service was satisfying.
reason.id: Ini salah. Wanita itu dengan jelas menyatakan, 'Makanannya enak.' Pilihan ini secara keliru mengklaim bahwa baik makanan maupun layanan tidak memuaskan.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you think that Bob really wanted me to go home?
(man) He wasn't kidding when he told you to leave.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT BOB?

options.text: He told his kids to leave.

reason.en: The man does not mention Bob telling his kids to leave. This is an incorrect inference.
reason.id: Pria itu tidak menyebutkan Bob menyuruh anak-anaknya pergi. Ini adalah kesimpulan yang salah.

options.text: He seriously wanted the woman to go.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, the man's response 'He wasn't kidding when he told you to leave' directly confirms that Bob was serious about wanting the woman to go home. 'Wasn't kidding' is a synonym for 'seriously'.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, respons pria itu 'He wasn't kidding when he told you to leave' secara langsung mengonfirmasi bahwa Bob serius ingin wanita itu pergi. 'Wasn't kidding' adalah sinonim dari 'seriously'.

options.text: He was joking when he told the woman to leave.

reason.en: The man explicitly states 'He wasn't kidding,' which directly contradicts the idea that Bob was joking. This is a common trap using negation.
reason.id: Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan 'He wasn't kidding,' yang secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa Bob bercanda. Ini adalah jebakan umum yang menggunakan negasi.

options.text: He left with the woman.

reason.en: The dialogue does not provide any information about Bob leaving with the woman. This is an unsupported inference.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak memberikan informasi apa pun tentang Bob yang pergi bersama wanita itu. Ini adalah kesimpulan yang tidak didukung.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 1 - Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(woman) Can we finish this project before clos ing time?
(man) If we work on nothing else.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The project will take all their effort.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the man's statement 'If we work on nothing else' implies that the project will require their full attention and effort, meaning it will take all their effort. This is a paraphrase of the original statement.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan pria 'If we work on nothing else' menyiratkan bahwa proyek tersebut akan membutuhkan perhatian dan upaya penuh mereka, yang berarti proyek tersebut akan menyita seluruh usaha mereka. Ini adalah parafrase dari pernyataan asli.

options.text: They have no other work to do.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The man's statement implies that *if* they dedicate themselves to the project, they *can* finish it. It doesn't state they have no other work; rather, it prioritizes this project above all else.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pernyataan pria tersebut menyiratkan bahwa *jika* mereka mendedikasikan diri untuk proyek tersebut, mereka *bisa* menyelesaikannya. Pernyataan itu tidak mengatakan bahwa mereka tidak punya pekerjaan lain; sebaliknya, itu memprioritaskan proyek ini di atas segalanya.

options.text: It's impossible to finish.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The man's statement is conditional ('If we work on nothing else'). It suggests that finishing is possible *if* they focus solely on it, not that it's impossible.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pernyataan pria tersebut bersifat kondisional ('If we work on nothing else'). Ini menunjukkan bahwa penyelesaiannya mungkin *jika* mereka hanya fokus padanya, bukan bahwa itu tidak mungkin.

options.text: They aren't even close to finishing the project.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The man's statement implies that finishing is achievable if they dedicate all their effort. It does not suggest they are far from finishing.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pernyataan pria tersebut menyiratkan bahwa penyelesaian dapat dicapai jika mereka mencurahkan seluruh upaya mereka. Pernyataan itu tidak menunjukkan bahwa mereka masih jauh dari penyelesaian.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues (Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms)

questions.passage:
(man) Would you mind staying an hour more?
(woman) I'd rather not.
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT STAYING?

options.text: She doesn't mind an hour more.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the woman explicitly states 'I'd rather not,' which is a polite way of saying she does not want to stay.
reason.id: Ini salah karena wanita itu secara eksplisit menyatakan 'I'd rather not,' yang merupakan cara sopan untuk mengatakan dia tidak ingin tinggal.

options.text: She'd rather stay more than an hour.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The phrase 'I'd rather not' means she prefers not to do the action suggested, which is staying an hour more. It does not imply she wants to stay longer than an hour.
reason.id: Ini salah. Frasa 'I'd rather not' berarti dia lebih memilih untuk tidak melakukan tindakan yang disarankan, yaitu tinggal satu jam lagi. Ini tidak menyiratkan bahwa dia ingin tinggal lebih dari satu jam.

options.text: It's better to stay than go.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The woman's response 'I'd rather not' directly contradicts this statement. She does not think it is better to stay.
reason.id: Ini salah. Respons wanita itu 'I'd rather not' secara langsung bertentangan dengan pernyataan ini. Dia tidak berpikir lebih baik untuk tinggal.

options.text: She prefers to leave.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement (and its opposite, disagreement), the phrase 'I'd rather not' is a polite way of expressing a preference against something. In this context, it means she prefers not to stay an hour more, implying she prefers to leave.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement (dan kebalikannya, ketidaksetujuan), frasa 'I'd rather not' adalah cara sopan untuk mengungkapkan preferensi yang menentang sesuatu. Dalam konteks ini, itu berarti dia lebih memilih untuk tidak tinggal satu jam lagi, menyiratkan bahwa dia lebih memilih untuk pergi.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues (Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement)

questions.passage:
(woman) Would you recommend the hotel where you stayed in New York?
(man) The hotel provided service that was unequaled!
questions.question: 10. WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT THE HOTEL?

options.text: The service at the hotel wasn't too good.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the man states the service was 'unequaled,' which means it was excellent, not bad.
reason.id: Ini salah karena pria itu menyatakan bahwa layanan itu 'tak tertandingi' (unequaled), yang berarti itu luar biasa, bukan buruk.

options.text: This hotel gave excellent service.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the man's statement 'service that was unequaled!' is a strong positive endorsement. 'Unequaled' means unmatched or superior, which is synonymous with 'excellent service'.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan pria itu 'layanan yang tak tertandingi!' adalah dukungan positif yang kuat. 'Tak tertandingi' (unequaled) berarti tak tertandingi atau unggul, yang sinonim dengan 'layanan luar biasa'.

options.text: The service at the hotel could have been improved.

reason.en: This is incorrect. 'Unequaled' implies the highest quality, not that it needed improvement.
reason.id: Ini salah. 'Tak tertandingi' menyiratkan kualitas tertinggi, bukan bahwa itu perlu perbaikan.

options.text: This hotel's service was the same as the service at other hotels.

reason.en: This is incorrect. 'Unequaled' specifically means it was NOT the same as other hotels; it was better.
reason.id: Ini salah. 'Tak tertandingi' secara khusus berarti itu TIDAK sama dengan hotel lain; itu lebih baik.

parts.title: SKILL 8: LISTEN FOR DOUBLE NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS

questions.passage:
(man) Do you think Ron Rogers will be elected?
(woman) Well, it's not completely impossible.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN IMPLY ABOUT RON?

options.text: He'll definitely be elected.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the woman's statement 'it's not completely impossible' is a double negative that implies uncertainty, not a definite outcome. It directly contradicts the idea of him 'definitely' being elected.
reason.id: Ini salah karena pernyataan wanita 'it's not completely impossible' adalah negatif ganda yang menyiratkan ketidakpastian, bukan hasil yang pasti. Ini secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa dia 'pasti' terpilih.

options.text: The election is now complete.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the dialogue is about the possibility of an election outcome, not about the election already being finished. The phrase 'will be elected' refers to a future event.
reason.id: Ini salah karena dialog tersebut adalah tentang kemungkinan hasil pemilihan, bukan tentang pemilihan yang sudah selesai. Frasa 'will be elected' mengacu pada peristiwa di masa depan.

options.text: She has high hopes for his chances.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The phrase 'not completely impossible' suggests a possibility, but it does not convey strong optimism or 'high hopes.' It's a cautious statement, not an enthusiastic one.
reason.id: Ini salah. Frasa 'not completely impossible' menunjukkan kemungkinan, tetapi tidak menyampaikan optimisme yang kuat atau 'harapan tinggi.' Ini adalah pernyataan yang hati-hati, bukan yang antusias.

options.text: It may happen.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, the woman uses a double negative ('not completely impossible') which is a way of expressing that something is possible, though perhaps not guaranteed. This implies that his election might happen.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, wanita tersebut menggunakan negatif ganda ('not completely impossible') yang merupakan cara untuk menyatakan bahwa sesuatu itu mungkin, meskipun mungkin tidak terjamin. Ini menyiratkan bahwa pemilihannya mungkin terjadi.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 7 - Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(woman) How was your tennis match today?
(man) I didn't serve well, and I didn't volley well either.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT HIS TENNIS GAME?

options.text: Both parts of his game were bad.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the man states he did not serve well and did not volley well either. This implies both aspects of his game were not good, which is paraphrased in option 0. The phrase 'didn't serve well' and 'didn't volley well either' are synonymous with 'both parts of his game were bad'.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pria tersebut menyatakan bahwa dia tidak melakukan servis dengan baik dan juga tidak melakukan voli dengan baik. Ini menyiratkan bahwa kedua aspek permainannya tidak bagus, yang diparafrasekan dalam pilihan 0. Frasa 'didn't serve well' dan 'didn't volley well either' sinonim dengan 'both parts of his game were bad'.

options.text: He served better than he volleyed.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The man states he did not serve well AND did not volley well. There is no indication that one was better than the other; both were stated as not good.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pria itu menyatakan bahwa dia tidak melakukan servis dengan baik DAN juga tidak melakukan voli dengan baik. Tidak ada indikasi bahwa salah satu lebih baik dari yang lain; keduanya dinyatakan tidak baik.

options.text: Some parts of his game were better than others.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The man explicitly states that he did not perform well in two specific areas ('didn't serve well,' 'didn't volley well either'). This suggests a lack of skill in multiple areas, not that some parts were better than others.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa dia tidak tampil baik dalam dua area spesifik ('didn't serve well,' 'didn't volley well either'). Ini menunjukkan kurangnya keterampilan di banyak area, bukan bahwa beberapa bagian lebih baik dari yang lain.

options.text: He played rather well.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The man's statement 'I didn't serve well, and I didn't volley well either' directly contradicts playing 'rather well.' This is a common trap where the answer is the opposite of what is stated.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pernyataan pria itu 'I didn't serve well, and I didn't volley well either' secara langsung bertentangan dengan bermain 'rather well.' Ini adalah jebakan umum di mana jawabannya adalah kebalikan dari apa yang dinyatakan.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 2 - Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(man) Was Gary prepared for the debate?
(woman) It is no surprise that he was unpre pared.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT GARY?

options.text: It is a surprise that he was prepared.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because it directly contradicts the woman's statement. The woman says 'It is no surprise that he was unprepared,' which means his lack of preparation was expected, not surprising.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena secara langsung bertentangan dengan pernyataan wanita tersebut. Wanita itu berkata 'Tidak mengejutkan bahwa dia tidak siap,' yang berarti kurangnya persiapan adalah hal yang diharapkan, bukan mengejutkan.

options.text: He was not ready, as usual.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, the woman's statement 'It is no surprise that he was unprepared' implies that Gary's lack of preparation was expected. The phrase 'no surprise' combined with 'unprepared' means it was not surprising that he was not ready. Option [1] 'He was not ready, as usual' captures this meaning, suggesting a habitual lack of readiness.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, pernyataan wanita 'Tidak mengejutkan bahwa dia tidak siap' menyiratkan bahwa Gary tidak siap adalah hal yang diharapkan. Frasa 'tidak mengejutkan' dikombinasikan dengan 'tidak siap' berarti tidak mengejutkan bahwa dia tidak siap. Pilihan [1] 'Dia tidak siap, seperti biasa' menangkap makna ini, menunjukkan kebiasaan kurangnya kesiapan.

options.text: He prepared a really big surprise.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because it misinterprets the word 'unprepared.' The woman is talking about Gary's lack of readiness for the debate, not about him planning a surprise.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena salah menafsirkan kata 'unprepared' (tidak siap). Wanita itu berbicara tentang kurangnya kesiapan Gary untuk debat, bukan tentang dia merencanakan kejutan.
